$comment: x86_64 target descriptor (plan 12 §2.6 ML5 cycle 2 pivot — config-driven targets). Parallels src/dss-config/sources/*.lang.json: a compile target is a config file the LIR substrate reads, NOT C++ code. Adding a new target = drop a new .target.json. Loaded via TargetSchema::loadShipped("x86_64").

target.name: x86_64
target.description: Minimal x86_64 opcode set for ML5 cycle 2. Cycle 3 (instruction selection) expands to full c-subset corpus coverage; cycle 2b adds the register file + ABI sections.
target.$reservedFields: The per-opcode minOperands/maxOperands/minSuccessors/maxSuccessors fields are documented in the JSON for completeness but are NOT yet enforced by the substrate — the LIR builder only checks terminator-ness against `isTerminator` today. Cycle 3 isel + the MIR verifier will start consuming the arity bounds; until then they are advisory metadata.

opcodes.$comment: Slot 0 = the Invalid sentinel. Every target must declare it as the first opcode so the substrate's `opcode == 0` check (in LirBuilder::addInst) catches default-constructed insts loudly.
